title: Listening - Part 4: Remote Working
description: You will hear five short extracts in which people talk about working from home. You must complete two tasks while listening.

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: I’ve been a freelance translator for years, but [21]the main reason I decided to set up a permanent home office was the sheer cost of the daily commute[/21]. I was spending a fortune on train tickets. It’s been great for my bank balance. However, [26]what I’ve really appreciated is being able to set my own hours[/26]. If I want to work at 10 PM and take a long walk at midday, I can. That autonomy is priceless to me now. || Speaker 2: Working from home was [22]initially a way for me to balance my job with looking after my young children[/22]. I didn't want to miss out on their early years. It’s been a juggle, for sure. But the real benefit has been [27]the massive boost in my actual productivity[/27]. Without the constant interruptions of an open-plan office, I get through twice as much work in half the time. || Speaker 3: I’m a graphic designer, and [23]I moved to a remote setup because I felt the office environment was stifling my creativity[/23]. I needed a space that was 'mine'. It’s worked wonders. [28]I also find that I’m much more willing to take creative risks now[/28] that I don't have a manager looking over my shoulder every five minutes. I feel much more empowered to try new things. || Speaker 4: My company went fully remote last year. [24]I was worried about the social isolation, but I actually chose it because it meant I could finally move out of the city[/24] to a place I could actually afford. It’s been a life-changer. [29]The sense of peace and quiet I have now is something I never thought I’d find[/29]. My stress levels have plummeted because I’m surrounded by trees instead of traffic. || Speaker 5: I’m a consultant, and [25]I went remote to reduce my carbon footprint[/25]. I was tired of the guilt of driving forty miles every day. It was a purely ethical decision. What I’ve gained, though, is [30]a much better relationship with my local community[/30]. I actually know my neighbours now because I’m around during the day, and I use the local shops. I feel much more connected to where I actually live.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21 – 25, choose from the list (A – H) why each speaker decided to work from home.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 mentions the 'sheer cost of the daily commute' and train tickets.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 wanted to 'balance my job with looking after my young children'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 felt the office was 'stifling my creativity'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 chose it so they could 'finally move out of the city' to a cheaper area.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 wanted to 'reduce my carbon footprint' and avoid driving.

options.F: to be able to afford a better home
options.G: to find a more inspiring workspace
options.H: to recover from a health issue

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26 – 30, choose from the list (A – H) what each speaker has gained most from the experience.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 values being 'able to set my own hours' and the autonomy.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 mentions a 'massive boost in my actual productivity'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 feels 'much more willing to take creative risks'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 values the 'sense of peace and quiet' and lower stress.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 feels 'much more connected to where I actually live' and knows neighbours.

options.C: stronger ties with the local area
options.G: the ability to work more efficiently
